title: Declarative XML Handling

Declarative XML Handling

Declarative XML Handling

Declarative XML handling is a simple way to write and read XML streams using Uniface-compliant DTDs, element-to-field mappings, and the xmlsave and xmlload ProcScript statements.

Uniface provides the following facilities for declarative XML handling:

  • xmlstream data type for fields and operation parameters.
  • Default mapping between Uniface data structures and XML—entities and fields are expressed as elements in XML.
  • DTD Editor that can be used to view and define DTDs used by XML streams. It can also be used to generate Uniface-compliant DTDs for declarative XML handling.
  • ProcScript statements for writing, reading, and validating Uniface-compliant XML streams—use xmlsave, xmlload, and xmlvalidate.
  • ProcScript statements reconnect and retrieve/reconnect to reconnect an XML stream containing disconnected record sets with their data source.

Uniface-Compliant XML Streams

For declarative XML handling, XML streams must comply to the Uniface XML structure, in which Uniface entities and fields are represented as XML elements and modification and validation status as XML attributes. Fields are represented as child elements of the entity’s element. Inner entities are represented as child elements of the outer entity. The structure of the XML stream (including the names, nesting, and order of all elements) is declared by a DTD, which is defined in the application model. DTDs can be generated from application model entities or from components.

Uniface can include processing information in the XML stream as XML attributes. These describe the modification and validation status of the occurrences, and enable the data to be reconnected to its source.

For more information, see XML Streams and Uniface XML Constructs.

ProcScript

The xmlsave ProcScript statement saves the data from fields (and their modification and validation state) on a component into an XML stream, converting it to comply with the XML 1.0 standard. The XML stream is stored in a variable or parameter with xmlstream data type.

The xmlload extracts data from an XML stream and places the data as in the component. Using a DTD and an element-to-field map, it converts element values into Uniface field values.

When converting dat between Uniface and XML, these ProcScript statements also automatically convert data types and substitute characters reserved for XML syntax, as required.

To validate Uniface-compliant XML streams, you can use the xmlvalidate ProcScript statement.

Related Links
  • Uniface XML Constructs

    Uniface enables you to use and manipulate data in a variety of ways, including Structs, Uniface XML streams, and component-based APIs, UXMLREADER and UXMLWRITER .
